target.state: uncommitted working tree (9 files changed, +563/-77, plus untracked spec and handoff)

findings.summary: Reference OHLCV family (src/alpha_system/features/families/ohlcv/family.py, ~719-869) still derives RTH/ETH membership from static canonical session_label; the repaired fast vwap_session_auction pack now diverges from its reference twin on real data (label statically ETH) for RTH-conditioned features (opening_range, rth/eth flags, overnight_range, anchored_vwap). Fixture parity passes only because fixture labels were aligned with timestamps. Spec-scoped residual, honestly recorded in the handoff Risks section.

Fast _template_trade_date uses local + polars.duration(days=1) (physical 24h) where reference uses calendar-date + timedelta(days=1); divergence only reachable for bars Sat 23:00-24:00 America/Chicago before a Sunday 02:00 DST shift, when CME index futures are closed. Recommend dt.offset_by('1d').

Labels close-out anchor (_is_close_out_terminal_candidate, fixed_horizon/family.py:962) treats 15:00:00 CT event_ts as RTH-eligible (inclusive) while the new feature truth is bar-start [08:30,15:00) exclusive; different objects (anchor eligibility vs segment membership), not a present contradiction, but must be carried into the recorded labels-adoption follow-up.

Unreachable final return in reference _rth_minutes_point (dead code); vwap fixture row 0 HALT->ETH removed the only non-RTH/ETH static-label scenario (halt truth lives in unchanged, separately tested halt_status_flag); no_overnight_range->no_overnight_trade expectation change is a correct consequence of that fixture change, exact-tuple assertion retained.

required_repairs: Follow-up phase (not in-phase rework): repoint families/ohlcv/family.py session-conditioned reference helpers at data.foundation.sessions.classify_session_timestamp before any reference recomputation or fast-vs-reference reconciliation of vwap_session_auction on real data is treated as authoritative, and before P28 vwap alpha specs rely on reference-engine RTH conditioning (W1).
Include the 15:00-inclusive close-out anchor boundary note (N1) in the recorded labels-adoption follow-up.
Optional: replace polars.duration(days=1) with dt.offset_by('1d') in fast _template_trade_date (W2).
